text: One user blocked, others fine → an allow/deny list in sshd config, not key permissions. Check the auth log during a failed attempt: `sudo tail -n 30 /var/log/auth.log` shows 'User deploy not allowed because not listed in AllowUsers'.

Find the directive: `sudo grep -rin 'allowusers\|denyusers\|allowgroups' /etc/ssh/sshd_config /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/`. It's `AllowUsers trainee` in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/access.conf` — a whitelist that omits `deploy`.

Add `deploy` to the list: `AllowUsers trainee deploy` (space-separated). Validate and reload: `sudo sshd -t && sudo service ssh reload` (or `sudo kill -HUP $(cat /run/sshd.pid)`). Retry the key login.
